━━ 📋 P L A N   D I S P A T C H ━━
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Planning worker wave dispatching.
Goal: Golden workflow test colony

Workers
  🔍 Scout Worker-XX  Survey the repo and distill planning findings from available territory reports
  📋 Route-Setter Worker-XX  Convert surveyed findings into an executable multi-phase colony plan

Coordination: .aether/data/spawn-tree.txt

── Dispatch ──
Planning Wave 1 starting (in-repo, serial)
  Execution: serial — single task in this wave
  🔍 Scout Worker-XX  Planning task: Survey the repo and distill planning findings from available territory reports

… 🔍 Scout Worker-XX  starting wave 1  Planning task: Survey the repo and distill planning findings from available territory reports

… 🔍 Scout Worker-XX  running wave 1  Planning task: Survey the repo and distill planning findings from available territory reports  [simulated worker heartbeat observed]

✓ 🔍 Scout Worker-XX  completed 0.0s  FakeInvoker completed task plan-0 for worker Worker-XX (caste: scout)

── Dispatch ──
Planning Wave 2 starting (in-repo, serial)
  Execution: serial — single task in this wave
  📋 Route-Setter Worker-XX  Planning task: Convert surveyed findings into an executable multi-phase colony plan

… 📋 Route-Setter Worker-XX  starting wave 2  Planning task: Convert surveyed findings into an executable multi-phase colony plan

… 📋 Route-Setter Worker-XX  running wave 2  Planning task: Convert surveyed findings into an executable multi-phase colony plan  [simulated worker heartbeat observed]

✓ 📋 Route-Setter Worker-XX  completed 0.0s  FakeInvoker completed task plan-1 for worker Worker-XX (caste: route_setter)

━━ 📋 P L A N ━━
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Scout and Route-Setter mapped the colony goal into executable phases.
Goal: Golden workflow test colony
Granularity: milestone (4-7 phases)
── Depth Selection ──
Planning depth: deep
Verification depth: heavy
Override: --planning-depth <light|standard|deep> --verification-depth <light|standard|heavy>
Plan size: 6 phases

Contract
  - Execution: 2 staged workers, scout then route-setter (2 waves), 2 workers
  - Timeouts: 15m0s worker max; Each planning worker gets its own timeout. The route-setter only runs after a completed scout stage; otherwise it becomes dependency_blocked.
  - Dependencies: Real worker dispatch requires an authenticated platform dispatcher. Route-setter execution depends on the scout completing first.
  - Fallback: If the scout or route-setter fails, blocks, or times out after dispatch starts, emit dispatch_mode=fallback and synthesize planning artifacts locally while preserving any real worker artifacts that landed first. Visibility: dispatch_mode, planning_warning, artifact_source, plan_source.
  - Coordination: .aether/data/spawn-tree.txt
  - Artifacts: .aether/data/planning/SCOUT.md, .aether/data/planning/ROUTE-SETTER.md, .aether/data/planning/phase-plan.json, .aether/data/phase-research

Planning Artifacts
  - SCOUT.md
  - ROUTE-SETTER.md

Phase Research
  - phase-1-research.md
  - phase-2-research.md
  - phase-3-research.md
  - phase-4-research.md
  - phase-5-research.md
  - ... and 1 more phase research files

Phase 1 — Contract and gap mapping
  Lock the expected ant-process behavior against the current Go command surface.
  Task 1.1
    Goal: Compare the documented ant workflow with the current Codex command behavior
    Constraints:
      - Keep the Go binary as the execution source of truth
      - Use Claude/OpenCode command specs as the external contract
    Hints:
      - .claude/commands/ant/colonize.md
      - .claude/commands/ant/plan.md
      - cmd/codex_workflow_cmds.go
    Success Criteria:
      - The next implementation slices are dependency-ordered
      - The parity gaps are explicit
  Task 1.2
    Goal: Decide the observable ant-process outputs Codex must emit during each core command
    Constraints:
      - Do not claim capabilities the Go binary does not actually perform
    Hints:
      - main.go
      - main_test.go
    Success Criteria:
      - Each command has a concrete dispatch contract
      - Spawn tree and artifacts are part of the contract
  Phase Success Criteria:
    - The parity contract is explicit
    - The colony has an honest execution target

Phase 2 — Colonize orchestration
  Deliver surveyor-driven territory mapping that writes usable survey artifacts and spawn records.
  Task 2.1
    Goal: Implement the surveyor workflow for Codex colonize
    Constraints:
      - Follow the repository's existing structure and conventions
      - Keep changes scoped to the current goal
      - No obvious technical debt markers detected.
    Hints:
      - .aether/data/survey/
      - cmd/codex_colonize.go
      - spawn-tree.txt
    Success Criteria:
      - Survey artifacts are written
      - Surveyor spawns are recorded and completed
  Task 2.2
    Goal: Keep survey artifacts stable enough for planning to consume
    Constraints:
      - Avoid polluting reports with cache or generated artifacts
    Hints:
      - PATHOGENS.md should only mention real repo concerns
    Success Criteria:
      - Noise from generated/cache files is excluded
      - Survey artifacts are reusable inputs for plan
  Phase Success Criteria:
    - Territory survey is reliable
    - Planning can trust the survey output

Phase 3 — Planning orchestration
  Add a scout plus route-setter planning pass that produces artifacts and a grounded phase plan.
  Task 3.1
    Goal: Implement a scout planning pass that summarizes survey findings into buildable guidance
    Constraints:
      - Planning must still work when survey docs are missing
      - Scout output must be persisted as a planning artifact
    Hints:
      - SCOUT.md
      - cmd/codex_plan.go
      - phase-research/
    Success Criteria:
      - Planning produces a scout artifact
      - The route-setter consumes scout output and survey context
  Task 3.2
    Goal: Generate a route-setter plan with task constraints, hints, and success criteria
    Constraints:
      - The first phase must become ready
      - The saved plan must match the displayed plan
    Hints:
      - COLONY_STATE.json
      - renderPlanVisual
    Success Criteria:
      - Plan generation is grounded in repo context
      - Spawn records show scout and route-setter activity
  Phase Success Criteria:
    - Plan generation is ant-driven
    - The colony has a grounded next phase

Phase 4 — Build orchestration
  Replace visual-only build dispatch with real worker execution slices and artifact handling.
  Task 4.1
    Goal: Define and implement the builder, watcher, and specialist work sequence for build
    Constraints:
      - Keep command behavior inside Go
      - Recorded spawns must reflect real work performed
    Hints:
      - .claude/commands/ant/build.md
      - cmd/codex_visuals.go
      - cmd/codex_workflow_cmds.go
    Success Criteria:
      - Build does more than set state
      - The spawn plan matches actual command behavior
  Task 4.2
    Goal: Persist build artifacts and phase context needed by continue
    Constraints:
      - Continue must not rely on implied work that never happened
    Hints:
      - main.go
      - main_test.go
    Success Criteria:
      - Continue can verify concrete outputs
      - Phase state stays consistent across reruns
  Phase Success Criteria:
    - Build behavior matches its visuals
    - Continue has real evidence to consume

Phase 5 — Continue orchestration
  Make continue perform the real verification and advancement work instead of only flipping statuses.
  Task 5.1
    Goal: Implement watcher-led verification and housekeeping before phase advancement
    Constraints:
      - Completed phases must only advance after verification
      - Signal housekeeping should stay wired into continue finalize
    Hints:
      - .claude/commands/ant/continue.md
      - cmd/signal_housekeeping.go
    Success Criteria:
      - Advance only happens after verification succeeds
      - Continue verifies actual artifacts
  Task 5.2
    Goal: Record the continue worker flow in state, spawn logs, and user-facing output
    Constraints:
      - The Next Up block must stay valid for the resulting state
    Hints:
      - print-next-up
      - renderContinueVisual
    Success Criteria:
      - Continue output matches actual work
      - The next phase becomes ready when appropriate
  Phase Success Criteria:
    - Continue is no longer a pure state flip
    - Phase advancement is defensible

Phase 6 — End-to-end verification
  Run the colony from init through seal and prove the Codex ant process is now honest.
  Task 6.1
    Goal: Add tests that prove colonize, plan, build, and continue record real worker activity
    Constraints:
      - Test the user path, not just helper functions
    Hints:
      - cmd/codex_colonize_test.go
      - main_test.go
    Success Criteria:
      - Core parity regressions are caught by tests
      - Spawn tree entries are asserted in tests
  Task 6.2
    Goal: Run a live colony loop and compare its outputs with the documented ant process
    Constraints:
      - Call out any remaining parity gap explicitly
    Hints:
      - .aether/data/survey/
      - COLONY_STATE.json
      - spawn-tree.txt
    Success Criteria:
      - Remaining gaps are small and explicit
      - The live loop proves the implemented parity
  Phase Success Criteria:
    - The live colony loop is credible
    - The remaining parity gap is narrow and testable

Coordination: .aether/data/spawn-tree.txt


━━ 🐜 N E X T   U P ━━
Run `aether build 1` to start the next planned phase.
Alternative: Run `aether focus "..."` or `aether redirect "..."` if you want to adjust the colony before the first wave.
